revision.comment: とりあえず版。鹿島信仰は書くことがかなりある
revision.text:
'''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は、関東・東北を中心として日本全国にある。これらは、[[鹿島神宮]]（[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]）の分社として創建されたもので、鹿島神宮と同じ[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]]を祀る。鹿島神宮の武甕槌神に対する信仰を'''鹿島信仰'''（かしましんこう）という。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた。
*[[鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|鹿嶋神社]]（[[福島県]][[白河市]]） *[[鹿島神社 (益子町)|鹿島神社]]（益子鹿島神社）（[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]） *[[鹿島神社 (那珂市)|鹿島神社]]（菅谷鹿島神社）（[[茨城県]][[那珂市]]） *[[鹿嶋神社 (高砂市)|鹿嶋神社]]（[[兵庫県]][[高砂市]]） *[[鹿島香取神社]]（[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]）
[[Category:神社 (祭神・信仰別)|かしましんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 概要 == 武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた。

revision.comment: 曖昧さ回避にしてみる
revision.text:
{{Aimai Top}} '''鹿島'''（'''嶋'''）'''神社'''（かしまじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は、[[関東地方|関東]]・[[東北地方]]を中心に[[日本]]全国に鎮座するが、多くは[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を[[勧請]]して創建され、鹿島神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた。
なお、鹿島神宮の武甕槌神に対する信仰を'''鹿島信仰'''（かしましんこう）という。
* [[鹿嶋神社 (白河市)]] - [[福島県]][[白河市]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (益子町)]] - [[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (那珂市)]] - [[茨城県]][[那珂市]]鎮座 * [[鹿嶋神社 (ひたちなか市)]] - 茨城県[[ひたちなか市]]鎮座 * [[鹿嶋神社 (品川区)]] - [[東京都]][[品川区]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (横浜市)]] - [[神奈川県]][[横浜市]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (相模原市)]] - 神奈川県[[相模原市]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (高岡市)]] - [[富山県]][[高岡市]]鎮座 * [[鹿嶋神社 (小諸市)]] - [[長野県]][[小諸市]]鎮座 * [[鹿島神社 (牧之原市)]] - [[静岡県]][[牧之原市]]鎮座 * [[鹿嶋神社 (高砂市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[高砂市]]鎮座 * [[鹿嶋神社 (香芝市)]] - [[奈良県]][[香芝市]]鎮座

{{Aimai Top}} '''鹿島'''（'''嶋'''）'''神社'''（かしまじんじゃ）という名前の[[神社]]は、[[関東地方|関東]]・[[東北地方]]を中心に[[日本]]全国に鎮座するが、多くは[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を[[勧請]]して創建され、鹿島神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた。
なお、鹿島神宮の武甕槌神に対する信仰を'''鹿島信仰'''（かしましんこう）という。

{{曖昧さ回避}} {{デフォルトソート:かしましんしや}} [[Category:鹿島神社|*]] [[Category:同名の神社]]

[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。
== 東北地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (一関市真柴)]]（[[岩手県]][[一関市]]真柴） * [[鹿嶋神社 (石巻市)]] <!--沢田駅の近く--> ([[宮城県]][[石巻市]]) * [[鹿島神社 (伊具郡)]] （宮城県[[伊具郡]]） * [[鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|鹿島大神宮]] （[[福島県]][[郡山市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|鹿嶋神社]] （福島県[[白河市]]）
== 関東地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (益子町)|鹿島神社]] （[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]） * [[鹿島神社 (栃木県栃木市藤岡町部屋)|鹿島神社]] （[[栃木県]][[栃木市]][[藤岡町部屋]]） * [[鹿島神社 (那珂市菅谷)|鹿島神社]] （[[茨城県]][[那珂市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (土浦市沖宿町)|鹿島神社]]（茨城県[[土浦市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (ひたちなか市)|鹿嶋神社]] （茨城県[[ひたちなか市]]） * [[助川鹿嶋神社]]（茨城県[[日立市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (勝浦市)|鹿島神社]] （[[千葉県]][[勝浦市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (品川区)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[東京都]][[品川区]]） * [[鹿島神社 (江戸川区)]] * [[鹿島神社 (横浜市)|鹿島神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (相模原市)|鹿島神社]] （神奈川県[[相模原市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (横須賀市)]]
== 中部地方 == * [[鹿嶋神社 (朝日町)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[富山県]][[下新川郡]][[朝日町 (富山県)|朝日町]]） * [[鹿島神社 (穴水町)|鹿島神社]] （[[石川県]][[鳳珠郡]][[穴水町]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (小諸市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[長野県]][[小諸市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (大町市)|鹿島神社]] （長野県[[大町市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (伊東市)|鹿島神社]] （[[静岡県]][[伊東市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (東伊豆町)|鹿島神社]] （静岡県[[東伊豆町]]奈良本(北川)） * [[鹿島神社 (牧之原市)|鹿島神社]] （静岡県[[牧之原市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (蟹江町)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[愛知県]][[海部郡 (愛知県)|海部郡]][[蟹江町]]）
== 近畿地方 == * [[鹿嶋神社 (高砂市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[高砂市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (香芝市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[奈良県]][[香芝市]]）
== 中国・四国地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (松山市)|鹿島神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]）
== 九州地方 == * [[鹿島神社（太宰府市）|鹿島神社]]（[[福岡県]][[太宰府市]]） * [[近津鹿島神社]] （[[熊本県]][[熊本市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (氷川町)|鹿島神社]]（[[熊本県]][[八代郡]][[氷川町]]）

'''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ、{{旧字体|鹿嶋神社}}）は、「鹿島」を社名に持ち[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神（タケミカヅチ）]]を祭神とする神社。
[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。
== 東北地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (一関市真柴)]]（[[岩手県]][[一関市]]真柴） * [[鹿嶋神社 (石巻市)]] <!--沢田駅の近く--> ([[宮城県]][[石巻市]]) * [[鹿島神社 (伊具郡)]] （宮城県[[伊具郡]]） * [[鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|鹿島大神宮]] （[[福島県]][[郡山市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|鹿嶋神社]] （福島県[[白河市]]）
== 関東地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (益子町)|鹿島神社]] （[[栃木県]][[芳賀郡]][[益子町]]） * [[鹿島神社 (栃木県栃木市藤岡町部屋)|鹿島神社]] （[[栃木県]][[栃木市]][[藤岡町部屋]]） * [[鹿島神社 (那珂市菅谷)|鹿島神社]] （[[茨城県]][[那珂市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (土浦市沖宿町)|鹿島神社]]（茨城県[[土浦市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (ひたちなか市)|鹿嶋神社]] （茨城県[[ひたちなか市]]） * [[助川鹿嶋神社]]（茨城県[[日立市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (勝浦市)|鹿島神社]] （[[千葉県]][[勝浦市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (品川区)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[東京都]][[品川区]]） * [[鹿島神社 (江戸川区)]] * [[鹿島神社 (横浜市)|鹿島神社]] （[[神奈川県]][[横浜市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (相模原市)|鹿島神社]] （神奈川県[[相模原市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (横須賀市)]]
== 中部地方 == * [[鹿嶋神社 (朝日町)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[富山県]][[下新川郡]][[朝日町 (富山県)|朝日町]]） * [[鹿島神社 (穴水町)|鹿島神社]] （[[石川県]][[鳳珠郡]][[穴水町]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (小諸市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[長野県]][[小諸市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (大町市)|鹿島神社]] （長野県[[大町市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (伊東市)|鹿島神社]] （[[静岡県]][[伊東市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (東伊豆町)|鹿島神社]] （静岡県[[東伊豆町]]奈良本(北川)） * [[鹿島神社 (牧之原市)|鹿島神社]] （静岡県[[牧之原市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (蟹江町)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[愛知県]][[海部郡 (愛知県)|海部郡]][[蟹江町]]）
== 近畿地方 == * [[鹿嶋神社 (高砂市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[兵庫県]][[高砂市]]） * [[鹿嶋神社 (香芝市)|鹿嶋神社]] （[[奈良県]][[香芝市]]）
== 中国・四国地方 == * [[鹿島神社 (松山市)|鹿島神社]] （[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]）
== 九州地方 == * [[鹿島神社（太宰府市）|鹿島神社]]（[[福岡県]][[太宰府市]]） * [[近津鹿島神社]] （[[熊本県]][[熊本市]]） * [[鹿島神社 (氷川町)|鹿島神社]]（[[熊本県]][[八代郡]][[氷川町]]）

'''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ、{{旧字体|鹿嶋神社}}）は、「鹿島」を社名に持ち[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神（タケミカヅチ）]]を祭神とする神社。
[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。

'''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ、{{旧字体|鹿嶋神社}}）は、「鹿島」を社名に持ち[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神（タケミカヅチ）]]を祭神とする神社。
[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。

[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。

'''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ、{{旧字体|鹿嶋神社}}）は、「鹿島」を社名に持ち[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神（タケミカヅチ）]]を祭神とする神社。
[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社}} and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''鹿島神社'''（かしまじんじゃ、{{旧字体|鹿嶋神社}}）は、「鹿島」を社名に持ち[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神（タケミカヅチ）]]を祭神とする神社。
[[東北|東北地方]]・[[関東地方]]を中心として全国に約600社あり、[[茨城県]][[鹿嶋市]]の[[鹿島神宮]]を総本社とする。多くは鹿島神宮から[[勧請]]して創建され、神宮と同じ[[武甕槌神]]を祀っている。
武甕槌神は、[[香取神宮]]（[[千葉県]][[香取市]]）の[[経津主神]]とともに[[中臣氏]]・[[藤原氏]]の[[氏神]]とされたことから朝廷・中央貴族の信仰を受け、武神であることから武家の崇敬も受けた（'''鹿島信仰'''）。

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}

'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').
==Tōhoku region== * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|ja|鹿島神社 (一関市真柴)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123398290}} (Mashiba, [[:en:Ichinoseki, Iwate|Ichinoseki, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506078}} ([[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja|鹿島神社 (伊具郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506080}} ([[:en:Igu District, Miyagi|Igu District, Miyagi]]) * {{ill|Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|lt=Kashima Daijingū|lt_ja=鹿島大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|1=Kashima Daijingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687197}} ([[:en:Kōriyama|Kōriyama, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|lt=Kashima Shrine|lt_ja=鹿嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506081}} ([[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]])

'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').
==Tōhoku region== * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|ja|鹿島神社 (一関市真柴)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q123398290}} (Mashiba, [[:en:Ichinoseki, Iwate|Ichinoseki, Iwate]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506078}} ([[:en:Ishinomaki|Ishinomaki, Miyagi]], [[:en:Miyagi Prefecture|Miyagi Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja|鹿島神社 (伊具郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506080}} ([[:en:Igu District, Miyagi|Igu District, Miyagi]]) * {{ill|Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|lt=Kashima Daijingū|lt_ja=鹿島大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|1=Kashima Daijingu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28687197}} ([[:en:Kōriyama|Kōriyama, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|lt=Kashima Shrine|lt_ja=鹿嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135506081}} ([[:en:Shirakawa, Fukushima|Shirakawa, Fukushima]])

'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity [[:en:Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi]].
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]] and the [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]], with the head shrine being [[:en:Kashima Shrine|Kashima Jingū]] in [[:en:Kashima, Ibaraki|Kashima, Ibaraki]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with [[:en:Futsunushi|Futsunushi]] of [[:en:Katori Shrine|Katori Jingū]] ([[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the [[:en:Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi clan]] and [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]]. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677154]] Wikidata name: Kashima Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:24, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1={{ill|Kashima Shrines|ja|鹿島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrines|qq=}}
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]]
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]]
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]]
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki|1=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū|1=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]]
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|WD=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|WD=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki|1=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|WD=Q1107286|WD=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|WD=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū|1=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|WD=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:鹿島神社]]
and {{ill|Katori Shrines|ja|香取神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori Shrines|qq=}} are not common enough to bother making all the articles but making these lists is good because I think a lot of westerners do not understand the sheer quantity of them[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:07, 24 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Kashima Shrine''' (鹿島神社, [[:en:Kyujitai|Kyujitai]]: 鹿嶋神社) is a type of Shinto shrine named "Kashima" dedicated to the deity {{ill|Takemikazuchi|en|Takemikazuchi|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}}.
There are approximately 600 Kashima Shrines nationwide, concentrated primarily in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} and the {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}}, with the head shrine being {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Jingū|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Kashima, Ibaraki|en|Kashima, Ibaraki|qid=Q467471|lt=Kashima, Ibaraki|1=Kashima, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. Most of these shrines were established by inviting the deity from Kashima Jingū, worshipping the same deity, Takemikazuchi.
Takemikazuchi, together with {{ill|Futsunushi|en|Futsunushi|qid=Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi|1=Futsunushi}} of {{ill|Katori Shrine|en|Katori Shrine|qid=Q372380|lt=Katori Jingū|1=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|qid=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|qid=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture|1=Chiba Prefecture}}), was revered by the Imperial court and central aristocracy as the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Nakatomi clan|en|Nakatomi clan|qid=Q1198447|lt=Nakatomi clan|1=Nakatomi clan}} and {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}. Being a god of martial prowess, Takemikazuchi was also worshipped by samurai families ('''Kashima belief''').
==Tōhoku region== * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|ja|鹿島神社 (一関市真柴)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Mashiba, Ichinoseki)|1=Kashima Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q123398290}} (Mashiba, {{ill|Ichinoseki, Iwate|en|Ichinoseki, Iwate|qid=Q390817|lt=Ichinoseki, Iwate|1=Ichinoseki, Iwate}}, {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|qid=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (石巻市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506078|1=Kashima Shrine (Ishinomaki)}} ({{ill|Ishinomaki|en|Ishinomaki|qid=Q270814|lt=Ishinomaki, Miyagi|1=Ishinomaki}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja|鹿島神社 (伊具郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Igu District)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506080|1=Kashima Shrine (Igu District)}} ({{ill|Igu District, Miyagi|en|Igu District, Miyagi|qid=Q1155531|lt=Igu District, Miyagi|1=Igu District, Miyagi}}) * {{ill|Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|ja|鹿島大神宮 (郡山市)|lt=Kashima Daijingū|lt_ja=鹿島大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Daijingū (Kōriyama)|1=Kashima Shrine (Fukushima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28687197}} ({{ill|Kōriyama|en|Kōriyama|qid=Q264206|lt=Kōriyama, Fukushima|1=Kōriyama}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}) * {{ill|Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja|鹿嶋神社 (白河市)|lt=Kashima Shrine|lt_ja=鹿嶋神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135506081|1=Kashima Shrine (Shirakawa)}} ({{ill|Shirakawa, Fukushima|en|Shirakawa, Fukushima|qid=Q750587|lt=Shirakawa, Fukushima|1=Shirakawa, Fukushima}})
